macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $77K as bond selloff deepens; crypto market stays cautious
items.summary: Bitcoin remained near $77,000 while the wider crypto market held around $2.57 trillion in value, even as global bond yields rose and risk appetite stayed mixed. The note also flagged continued weakness in spot demand and sustained net selling across recent sessions.

items.title: Major cryptos rise after Trump comments ease Iran tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin reportedly gained after President Donald Trump's remarks on Iran helped calm geopolitical concerns. The move came amid broader market relief as investors reacted to reduced near-term conflict risk.

items.title: Crypto market sees $159 million in liquidations over 24 hours
items.summary: A 24-hour liquidation wave hit the crypto market, with Bitcoin and Ethereum among the hardest affected on both long and short positions. The event highlights continued leverage risk and short-term volatility across major tokens.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google I/O 2026: Major AI push includes new Gemini, agentic tools, and XR eyewear
items.summary: Google’s I/O event reportedly showcased a broad set of AI updates, including Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini Omni, Antigravity 2.0, Gemini Spark, new Search agents, and Android XR intelligent eyewear. The announcements point to Google emphasizing multimodal generation, personal assistants, and agent-based workflows across consumer and developer products.

items.title: Google I/O highlights: Omni, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Antigravity 2.0, and Spark
items.summary: A separate recap of Google I/O says the biggest themes were multimodal creation and agentic tools, with Omni positioned for any-input-to-any-output generation and Gemini 3.5 Flash focused on speed and stronger coding/agent performance. The event also featured Google Flow, Google Pix, and other product updates aimed at consumers and creators.

items.title: Google I/O points to a split in AI strategy toward consumer agents and creative tools
items.summary: Another analysis of Google’s announcements suggests the company is leaning into a different AI path than some rivals, prioritizing broad consumer-facing use cases, multimodal input/output, and creative generation. It also notes SynthID adoption and new Gemini model benchmarks as part of the broader push.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass confirms June 2025 Wave 1 lineup
items.summary: Microsoft has revealed the first wave of games coming to Xbox Game Pass in June 2025, ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase. The lineup announcement is one of the biggest subscription-service updates in the latest gaming news cycle.

items.title: Helldivers 2 announces the Force of Law Warbond
items.summary: Arrowhead’s cooperative shooter is getting a new Force of Law Warbond, giving players another themed content drop. The reveal adds to the steady stream of live-service updates in the past day.

items.title: Square Enix unveils Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles Collector’s Box
items.summary: Square Enix revealed a Collector’s Box for Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles, though the bundle notably does not include the game itself. The announcement drew attention for its unusual contents and premium packaging.

items.title: Ex-Call of Duty and XDefiant producer says he is stepping away from gaming
items.summary: The executive producer behind XDefiant said he will be leaving the gaming industry for good as the game winds down after just one year. The comment marks a notable personnel change tied to a recent live-service shutdown.

items.title: Sony State of Play brings multiple major game reveals
items.summary: Sony’s latest State of Play featured several noteworthy announcements, including a new controller, a returning stealth franchise for VR, and new entries in Bloodstained and Nioh. The showcase also included a new Marvel fighting game from Arc System Works and Sony Interactive Entertainment.

items.title: Fortnite teases another free skin with a catch
items.summary: Epic Games surprised players with news of another free Fortnite skin, though the promotion comes with some conditions. It is one of the more talked-about battle royale updates from the past 24 hours.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announce Nioh 3
items.summary: Nioh 3 was officially announced during Sony’s State of Play, giving soulslike fans a new entry to look forward to. The reveal also confirmed that players can dive in immediately through a playable release or trial announcement tied to the showcase.

items.title: Pokémon GO brings back Community Day voting for August 2025
items.summary: Niantic has reopened Community Day voting for Pokémon GO’s August 2025 event, with four Pokémon in contention. The return of voting gives players a direct role in shaping one of the game’s biggest monthly events.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon expanded its AI assistant beyond devices by launching Alexa+ on the web, a move aimed at deeper agentic workflows and stronger competition with ChatGPT and Google Gemini.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold
items.summary: Samsung debuted its most advanced foldable yet, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that folds into a pocketable form factor.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to scale Android from mobile devices to desktops, potentially reshaping personal computing and challenging Microsoft and Apple in the PC market.

items.title: Quantum Motion builds first full-stack quantum computer on standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone that could improve the path to scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm introduced its new wearable chip platforms, focusing on battery life, design flexibility, and connectivity improvements to address key barriers to wider smartwatch adoption.
